title: Monthly dep hygiene sweep across all repos

brief.why: Unused deps accumulate silently — only caught when a CVE triggers Dependabot. Proactive monthly sweep catches dead weight before it matters.
brief.what: Todoist recurring task (monthly) to run github-cleanup Phase 4c across ~/Repos. Could also be a cron job that opens a PR.
brief.done: Recurring reminder exists and has fired at least once

done_note: Audit: closing — cross-repo concern, not garde-manger scope

title: Show stale indicator in search results

brief.why: Stale sources appear in search without indication that their path is invalid, causing confusion
brief.what: Add [stale] marker to search output for sources with status=stale
brief.done: Search results show [stale] indicator, optionally filter with --include-stale or --exclude-stale

title: Garde indexes Amp threads as a first-class source

brief.why: Amp is thread-focused — every conversation is a discrete, retrievable thread with an ID and URL. Unlike CC sessions (scattered JSONL in ~/.claude/projects/), Amp threads are structured and easy to enumerate. garde should parse them all, not just rely on close-ritual extractions.
brief.what: 1. Identify where Amp stores thread data on disk (or API) 2. Write an Amp adapter in src/garde/adapters/ that enumerates and parses threads 3. Extract content suitable for FTS indexing 4. Add --source amp to CLI choices 5. Test with real Amp threads
brief.done: uv run garde scan --source amp indexes Amp threads. uv run garde search finds content from Amp conversations. uv run garde recent --all shows Amp threads alongside CC sessions.

title: In-session extraction at /close

brief.why: Backfill is a catch-up operation that spawns subprocess per source. Extracting during /close uses tokens already being spent, has richer context, and eliminates the backfill queue entirely.
brief.what: 1. Add store-extraction call to /close skill after handoff write 2. Use HYBRID_EXTRACTION_PROMPT in-context (no subprocess needed) 3. Pipe result to mem store-extraction 4. Test with a real /close cycle
brief.done: New sessions get extractions at close time. Backfill queue stays empty without manual runs.

title: Audit repos without committed lock files

brief.why: Repos with pyproject.toml but no uv.lock in git mean Dependabot can't scan them — a blind spot in the security posture.
brief.what: Scan all spm1001 repos for presence of uv.lock/package-lock.json. Flag repos that have manifests but no lock file committed.
brief.done: List produced, lock files committed where appropriate

done_note: Audit: closing — one-time audit, not garde-manger scope

title: Automate runtime-engine detection in dep audit

brief.why: Current dep audit relies on prose advice to check for openpyxl/lxml/kaleido before removing. A script would be more robust and prevent accidental breakage.
brief.what: Script that greps source for string references like 'lxml', 'openpyxl', write_image, asyncio_mode before confirming a dep is truly unused. Integrate into github-cleanup Phase 4c.
brief.done: Script exists, tested against writing-style-and-criticism (lxml) and itv-linkedin-analytics (openpyxl, kaleido)

title: Backfill skips sessions that have handoff extractions

brief.why: Backfill spends ~30s of Opus time per session, billed against Max subscription. Sessions with handoffs already have free extractions via section parse, but backfill doesn't know — different source IDs (claude_code:uuid vs handoff:stem). We're burning tokens extracting what we already have.
brief.what: 1. Store session_id as metadata on handoff sources during scan 2. Modify backfill query to skip claude_code sources where a handoff extraction exists for the same session_id 3. Test with real data — verify token savings
brief.done: Backfill only processes sessions that have no handoff. Token spend drops proportionally to handoff coverage.
brief.how: In backfill's query, LEFT JOIN to check whether a handoff extraction exists for the same session. Match via session UUID: the handoff stem often contains the session ID (e.g., 2026-04-05-37cade0a matches claude_code:37cade0a-...). Alternatively, add a session_id column to handoff sources at scan time for clean joins. The handoff adapter already parses session_id from the preamble.

title: Claude_code adapter produces clean searchable text

brief.why: L1 search quality is poor — full_text() is a naive join that doesn't handle compaction boundaries, duplicate message IDs, system tag stripping, or meta injection filtering. ccconv in trousse handles all of these. Users searching for 'flamingoes' may miss sessions where the word appeared after a compaction boundary.
brief.what: 1. Extract ccconv's parse_session, merge_assistant_entries, build_turns, extract_human_text, extract_assistant_content into a shared module 2. Update garde's claude_code adapter to use shared module for full_text() 3. Re-scan existing claude_code sources to fix raw_text quality 4. Update ccconv CLI to import from shared module
brief.done: garde search finds terms that appear anywhere in a conversation, not just in the first few messages or non-compacted sections. ccconv and garde use identical JSONL parsing.
brief.how: Share ccconv's JSONL parsing with garde's claude_code adapter. Options: (a) extract ccconv's core functions into a shared Python module both can import, (b) have the adapter call ccconv as a subprocess, (c) refactor ccconv into a library with CLI wrapper. Option (a) is cleanest — a new package in batterie that both trousse and garde depend on. This is also the 'one place for CC JSONL parsing, multiple consumers' design the user asked for.

title: Dolt replaces file-based storage for cross-machine tools

brief.why: garde uses SQLite (can't sync without rsync + manual merge), bon uses JSONL (union merge breaks on same-item edits, session paths are machine-specific). Both need multi-machine coordination that git can't cleanly provide. Dolt gives row-level merge, push/pull on structured data, and audit trail for free.
brief.what: 1. Set up DoltHub repo (spm1001/garde-memory) 2. Migrate garde database.py from sqlite3 to MySQL client (Dolt-compatible) 3. Rewrite FTS5 queries to MySQL FULLTEXT (no table aliases in MATCH) 4. Prototype bon on Dolt — items table replaces items.jsonl, tactical.session becomes a column filter 5. Wire aboyeur to query Dolt for cross-machine bon state 6. Deploy clones on hezza, kube, Mac
brief.done: garde scan on any machine pushes to DoltHub, other machines pull and search. bon work/step on one machine visible from another without git push. Aboyeur can query active tacticals across all machines.

title: Rsync sessions, handoffs, Amp threads, and pending extractions onto hezza

brief.why: Hezza has 1 week of data; tube has months of CC sessions + handoffs, kube has Amp threads
brief.what: 1. Check disk space on hezza 2. Rsync CC sessions from tube 3. Rsync handoffs from tube 4. Rsync pending extractions from tube 5. Rsync Amp threads from kube
brief.done: All historical data present on hezza, verified by file counts

steps: Check disk space on hezza
Rsync CC sessions from tube
Rsync handoffs from tube
Rsync pending extractions from tube
Rsync Amp threads from kube

title: Search and drill work end-to-end for Amp threads

brief.why: Indexing is useless if search doesn't surface Amp content and drill can't display it
brief.what: 1. Verify FTS5 search returns Amp thread results 2. Verify garde drill loads Amp thread content 3. Verify garde recent --all shows Amp threads alongside CC sessions 4. Fix any gaps in display/formatting
brief.done: uv run garde search finds Amp content, uv run garde drill amp:T-xxx shows thread, uv run garde recent --all interleaves Amp and CC

steps: Verify FTS5 search returns Amp thread results
Verify garde drill loads Amp thread content
Verify garde recent --all shows Amp threads alongside CC sessions

title: Amp adapter parses threads into indexable sources

brief.why: Amp stores 61 threads as JSON files — structured, easy to enumerate, but invisible to garde
brief.what: 1. Create src/garde/adapters/amp.py with AmpSource dataclass and discover_amp generator 2. Parse thread JSON: extract messages, filter thinking/tool blocks, build full_text 3. Extract metadata (title, agentMode, project from env.initial.trees, token usage) 4. Handle presummary: Amp threads have no pre-distilled summary, so has_presummary=False
brief.done: discover_amp yields AmpSource for each thread in ~/.local/share/amp/threads/, full_text returns conversation content, source_id is amp:{thread_id}

title: stage-extraction.sh available on kube

brief.why: Without it, /close can't pre-stage extraction JSON — falls back to spawning a claude -p subprocess which is slower and may not be available on all machines
brief.what: 1. Check if stage-extraction.sh is part of trousse setup or garde install 2. Ensure it's deployed to kube via install.sh or symlink 3. Verify the staged extraction path works end-to-end on kube
brief.done: Running ~/.claude/scripts/stage-extraction.sh on kube succeeds. /close pre-staging works without fallback.

done_note: Audit: script exists in repo, deployment is ops not a bon item

brief.done: All existing tests pass plus new subagent tests, no regressions

title: Built ccconv CLI — canonical CC transcript parser

brief.why: Every Claude reinvents jq patterns to parse CC JSONL transcripts. Guéridon (CC-EVENTS.md, parseSessionJSONL) and garde (ClaudeCodeSource.full_text) both have working implementations but they are library-internal. A standalone CLI would let any Claude or script extract conversation from noise in one command.
brief.what: 1. Extract the 5-line algorithm from garde claude_code.py adapter. 2. Wrap as PEP 723 uv run --script CLI. 3. Support flags: --with-tools, --with-thinking, --last N, --json. 4. Strip system-reminder/task-notification XML tags. 5. Handle both on-disk JSONL and cloud session JSON formats. 6. Install to ~/.local/bin/ccconv via uv tool install.
brief.done: ccconv installed, tested against a real session, documented in garde CLAUDE.md

done_note: Completed previously — ccconv CLI was built and is functional

title: Verify claude -p auth works from cron on kube

brief.why: Daily backfill at 03:00 calls claude -p for LLM extraction. If Max subscription auth isn't in cron's environment, extractions fail silently to /tmp/garde-amp-backfill.log
brief.what: 1. Check /tmp/garde-amp-backfill.log after first 03:00 run 2. If auth fails, add CLAUDE_AUTH or equivalent to cron environment 3. Verify at least one Amp thread gets extracted
brief.done: garde-amp-backfill.log shows successful extractions, garde drill on a backfilled amp thread shows rich summary

title: garde-manger finds all subagent sessions and consolidates data on hezza

brief.why: Adapter has two bugs: glob too shallow (misses subagents/) and source_id collides for agents. Hezza only has 173 sessions vs thousands on tube/kube.
brief.what: 1. Fix source_id to use agent_id for subagents 2. Fix glob depth to **/*.jsonl 3. Harden is_agent detection 4. Store parent_session_id in metadata 5. Write tests 6. Rsync from tube and kube 7. Full scan and backfill 8. Set up cron automation
brief.done: garde status shows thousands of CC sources including subagents, Amp threads from kube, backfill running, cron scheduled

done_note: All 4 phases complete: adapter fixed (A), data synced from tube+kube (B), 6561 sources indexed with backfill running (C), cron set up (D)

title: Garde links Amp handoff chains into navigable arcs

brief.why: Amp threads have bidirectional parent/child relationships forming work chains (e.g. 5-thread Arc-to-Bon rename). The adapter now stores these in metadata['relationships'] but garde doesn't use them yet — each thread is still an island in search results
brief.what: 1. Parse relationships from amp source metadata during indexing 2. Store thread-to-thread edges in a new table or existing entity system 3. Surface chains in search results (e.g. 'part 3 of 5 in arc') 4. Enable garde drill to show chain context
brief.done: garde search shows chain membership, garde drill on a mid-chain thread shows parent and child links, full chain navigable

done_note: Won't do — Amp dropped from active tooling, no longer generating handoff chains

brief.done: pytest tests/test_amp.py passes, covers happy path and edge cases (empty thread, missing title, thread with relationships)
